message:
ci: Add GitHub Actions workflow for workerd integration tests

Builds python.wasm from source on Ubuntu (zig cc → wasm32-wasi),
generates stdlib-fs.ts, then runs 42 vitest tests inside workerd
via @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers.

Also fixes build-phase2.sh for Linux:
- Detect BUILD_TRIPLE from uname (was hardcoded to apple-darwin)
- Cross-platform sed -i via sedi() helper (macOS vs GNU sed)
- Fix make target (python vs python.exe)

Caches CPython source and python.wasm build to speed up subsequent runs.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+name: Test
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main]
+  pull_request:
+    branches: [main]
+
+jobs:
+  test:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 30
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "20"
+
+      - name: Install system dependencies
+        run: |
+          sudo apt-get update
+          sudo apt-get install -y build-essential
+
+      - name: Install zig
+        uses: mlugg/setup-zig@v2
+        with:
+          version: "0.15.2"
+
+      - name: Install wasmtime
+        run: |
+          curl https://wasmtime.dev/install.sh -sSf | bash
+          echo "$HOME/.wasmtime/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
+
+      - name: Install binaryen (wasm-opt)
+        run: |
+          BINARYEN_VERSION=122
+          curl -L "https://github.com/WebAssembly/binaryen/releases/download/version_${BINARYEN_VERSION}/binaryen-version_${BINARYEN_VERSION}-x86_64-linux.tar.gz" | tar xz
+          sudo mv binaryen-version_${BINARYEN_VERSION}/bin/* /usr/local/bin/
+
+      - name: Cache CPython source
+        uses: actions/cache@v4
+        with:
+          path: cpython
+          key: cpython-3.13.0-source
+
+      - name: Clone CPython
+        run: |
+          if [ ! -d cpython ]; then
+            git clone --depth 1 --branch v3.13.0 https://github.com/python/cpython.git cpython
+          fi
+
+      - name: Cache python.wasm build
+        uses: actions/cache@v4
+        with:
+          path: |
+            build/zig-wasi/python.wasm
+            build/zig-wrappers/
+          key: python-wasm-${{ hashFiles('scripts/build-phase2.sh', 'scripts/config.site-wasi', 'lib/wasi-shims/**', 'lib/pymode-imports/**') }}
+
+      - name: Build python.wasm
+        run: |
+          if [ ! -f build/zig-wasi/python.wasm ]; then
+            ./scripts/build-phase2.sh
+          fi
+          cp build/zig-wasi/python.wasm worker/src/python.wasm
+
+      - name: Generate stdlib-fs.ts
+        run: ./scripts/generate-stdlib-fs.sh
+
+      - name: Install npm dependencies
+        run: npm install
+
+      - name: Run tests (workerd)
+        run: npm test

@@ -49,7 +49,7 @@ if [ -z "$NATIVE_PYTHON" ]; then
     cd "$CPYTHON_DIR"
     make distclean 2>/dev/null || true
     ./configure --prefix="$ROOT_DIR/build/native/install"
-    make -j"$(sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || nproc)" python.exe
+    make -j"$(sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 2)"
     NATIVE_PYTHON="$CPYTHON_DIR/python.exe"
     if [ ! -x "$NATIVE_PYTHON" ]; then
         NATIVE_PYTHON="$CPYTHON_DIR/python"
@@ -152,7 +152,12 @@ cd "$BUILD_DIR"
 rm -rf "$BUILD_DIR"/*
 rm -f "$BUILD_DIR"/../config.cache 2>/dev/null
 
-BUILD_TRIPLE="$(uname -m)-apple-darwin"
+# Detect build triple for cross-compilation
+case "$(uname -s)" in
+    Darwin) BUILD_TRIPLE="$(uname -m)-apple-darwin" ;;
+    Linux)  BUILD_TRIPLE="$(uname -m)-pc-linux-gnu" ;;
+    *)      BUILD_TRIPLE="$(uname -m)-unknown-$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')" ;;
+esac
 
 CONFIG_SITE="$ROOT_DIR/scripts/config.site-wasi" \
 PKG_CONFIG=false \
@@ -198,21 +203,30 @@ PKG_CONFIG=false \
     py_cv_module__tkinter=n/a \
     py_cv_module__scproxy=n/a
 
+# Cross-platform sed -i (macOS requires '' arg, Linux does not)
+sedi() {
+    if [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
+        sed -i '' "$@"
+    else
+        sed -i "$@"
+    fi
+}
+
 # Step 3b: Patch pyconfig.h - use CPython pthread types instead of musl
 info "Patching pyconfig.h for WASI..."
-sed -i '' 's/^#define HAVE_PTHREAD_H 1/\/* #undef HAVE_PTHREAD_H *\//' "$BUILD_DIR/pyconfig.h"
+sedi 's/^#define HAVE_PTHREAD_H 1/\/* #undef HAVE_PTHREAD_H *\//' "$BUILD_DIR/pyconfig.h"
 
 # Step 3b2: Register _pymode as a built-in module in config.c
 # This makes `import _pymode` work without needing shared library loading.
 info "Registering _pymode built-in module..."
 CONFIG_C="$BUILD_DIR/Modules/config.c"
 if [ -f "$CONFIG_C" ] && ! grep -q "PyInit__pymode" "$CONFIG_C"; then
     # Add extern declaration before the ADDMODULE MARKER 1
-    sed -i '' '/\/\* -- ADDMODULE MARKER 1 --/i\
+    sedi '/\/\* -- ADDMODULE MARKER 1 --/i\
 extern PyObject* PyInit__pymode(void);
 ' "$CONFIG_C"
     # Add entry to _PyImport_Inittab before the ADDMODULE MARKER 2
-    sed -i '' '/\/\* -- ADDMODULE MARKER 2 --/i\
+    sedi '/\/\* -- ADDMODULE MARKER 2 --/i\
     {"_pymode", PyInit__pymode},
 ' "$CONFIG_C"
     info "  _pymode registered in config.c"
